explanation: The phrase 'Any failure of the gas supply' translates directly to the atomic proposition "gasSupplyFailure". The condition 'allows the patient to inhale and exhale unimpeded' means that both the inspiratory valve and the expiratory valve must be open for free airflow. This maps to the conjunction "inValveOpen & outValveOpen". The phrase 'shall leave the system in a state that allows...' indicates that upon a gas supply failure, the system must eventually (F) transition to and then permanently remain in (G) this unimpeded state. Thus, the consequence is F G (inValveOpen & outValveOpen). Finally, the word 'Any' implies that this property must hold globally (G) for all instances of gas supply failure, leading to the overall implication G(gasSupplyFailure -> F G (inValveOpen & outValveOpen)).
